description.value:
Source: Ariadne's Book of Legends - Alpha v1.2, p. 11-12.

Progression

Unlike their non-legendary counterparts, characters beyond 20th level do not simply advance in their progression for virtue of completing an adventure or gaining enough experience. Each legendary level is an enormous hurdle to overcome, one that some people simply cannot ever surpass. Many epic NPCs have reached the heights of their potential, the absolute limit of their growth, somewhere beyond 17th level. Some may have trained for decades, even centuries, to gain a single Legendary Level. The characters, the true heroes of the story, have infinite potential, of course… but each level beyond the 20th must be earned through feats unparalleled. Legendary Levels are awarded at the DM’s discretion, in moments that align with the game’s narrative.

Any level beyond the 20th is a good place to stop, depending on the scope of your game and the willingness of your DM. Just because there are 25 levels doesn’t mean you are obligated to reach them. Indeed, parties of the Ascended tier struggle to find much challenge in anything beyond the most powerful Legendary Foes ever conceived.

Final Boss. The DM might have selected a specific Legendary Foe as the final encounter of the campaign. Depending on which tier the foe is from, it can mark the expected ceiling of the adventure. An Ascended-tier boss, recommended for levels 24 and 25, can be used to fight a 23rd-level party, as a way to make the final battle feel even more deadly. In fact, depending on the number of players at your table, it may be wise to bump up the difficulty of some of these battles to maintain tension.

20th-Level Adventure. Some stories never go beyond 20th level. Perhaps your campaign is nearing the final stretch, and this book is here only to guide you through high-level combat and milestones the players can achieve for the final battle. Heroic Paths, Mythical Techniques, 10th-level spells, and Legendary Class features can serve to inspire a non-Legendary Campaign, giving specific power-ups as boons from Gods or features from lost artifacts without the framework of Legendary Levels. Using a Legend-tier Foe (recommended for 20th-21st level) as a Final Boss against a properly equipped 17th or 18th level party is a valid way of playing a Legendary Campaign! This manual is here to inspire, and only you know what is best for your specific table; no need to follow every rule!

Leveling Up

If you are expecting to level up regularly during your campaign, we recommend that each increase in power be marked by a Legendary Achievement, along the lines of the following examples.

External. The character discovers a powerful relic, inherits the powers of a legendary foe, or is blessed by a God. These power increases are often the culmination of an adventure–an external, immediate increase in

power gained through the adoption of a boon that exists in your world. These are the best ways to level up a Legendary Party–having that increase in power be extraordinary, not the result of simple training and adventuring.

Internal. The character hones their craft, whatever that may be, which results in an increase in level. An archmage discovers new spells beyond 9th level, a sword saint masters Mythical Techniques lost to memory and just recently recovered, a monk learns to walk on air. These are often accompanied by some external stimuli–discovering an ancient text depicting a 10th-level spell, for example–but occur internally and take time and effort to complete. The first few Legendary Levels are best for this kind of level-up. Internal increases must always have a built-in reason to occur! A constant of effort, dedication, and training on the part of the character.

Downtime. Over several years, decades, or even centuries of downtime, a Legendary Character may perform mythical acts that increase their level. They may have completed legendary adventures that will go down in history as folk tales, discovered new laws of reality in their isolated towers, or perfected their techniques beyond their previous limits. These moments between adventures are perfect for a Legendary Character to increase their level.

Maximum Level

If a party ever reaches 25th level, it becomes very hard to challenge the players with anything short of a God. Even in this book, the creatures that stand ready to face such a group are the minority. We recommend only hitting max level in the last arc, in the epic ramp-up toward the campaign finale. 25th level is, to this book, what 20th level is for regular D&D!

Edition Compatibility This book is written for both 5e and 5.5e. Terminology generally follows 5.5e convention for player-facing rules, while monster stat blocks lean closer to classic 5e design.

Where the two editions differ, the text will note which rule applies to which version. Watch out for 5.5e or 5e paragraphs, noted by their edition in bold letters. Some features only exist in one edition–the increasing Rage count for the Barbarian, for example, only matters in 5.5e, since 5e Barbarians have an unlimited number of rages at 20th level.

Certain terms may be unfamiliar depending on which edition you play. The Bloodied condition, for instance, is a 5.5e term referring to a creature that has fewer than half its total hit points remaining.

HERE BE LEGENDS

Summary

This book is divided into three great parts that include multiple chapters each:

The introductory section you are reading now, which settles the bases for a Legendary Campaign and explains the overarching systems and philosophy that permeate the book.

The Player section, from Chapter I to Chapter IV, will give players the tools to create legendary characters. +

The DM section, from Chapter IV to Chapter VIII, lists the many monsters, relics, and additional information a director needs to run a Legendary Campaign.

An Introduction: Here be Legends

This section contains general and special rules, along with our design philosophy and recommendations to run and play a Legendary Campaign. Chapter I: Legendary Characters

This chapter outlines the legendary level progression (2025) for each of the 12 core classes of D&D 5e / 5.5e. Soon, the Shaper and Starblade, our original classes, will be added to the list. Chapter II: Heroic Paths

This chapter introduces you to Heroic Paths, a progression system in which you pick from a wide selection of powerful feats, allowing you to customize your Legendary Character. These feats are organized in paths, which work somewhat like subclasses, with each feature building upon previous ones. Chapter III: Mythical Techniques

Martial classes have long been outscaled by learned wizards, ancient druids, and ascendant sorcerers, especially in the higher levels of D&D. No longer! This chapter focuses on Mythical Techniques, powerful abilities unlocked at certain legendary levels that further help you customize your martial character, allowing them to compete with the incredible might of epic magic. Chapter IV: Epic Magic

Magic beyond 9th level is the ultimate show of power. Both characters and foes will find themselves using their legendary resources to conjure the most powerful arcanisms in existence: 10th-level spells. This chapter lists all spells we’ve created to accompany our legendary system of play. Chapter V: The Legendary Game

In all our years playing epic-level D&D, we’ve accrued some good know-how about directing such mythical games. This chapter will be your guide on maintaining tension, running encounters challenging enough for Legendary Characters, and preparing the stakes for the world-shattering battles that will take place in your world.

HERE BE LEGENDS

Chapter VI: Legendary Foes

A campaign is nothing without its antagonist. This chapter is focused on our menagerie of Legendary Foes, creatures beyond CR 30 that have been specifically designed to challenge even the most powerful of parties. Lore, mechanics, lairs, and DM notes, this chapter will teach you to run these foes at your table. Chapter VII: Relics (Not In Alpha)

There are items more powerful than artifacts in the cosmos. This chapter will describe relics that can be acquired after defeating each of our Legendary Foes, along with some additional iconic magic items from the Universe of Strings. Chapter VIII: Appendices (Not In Alpha)

This final chapter will contain additional lore, easy-toreference tables, generic monsters for each Legendary Tier, and additional tools to help you homebrew your own Legendary Content.

source.custom: Original en-US text from Alpha v1.2
